channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will headline a rally in Atlanta a week before the Nov. 5 election alongside celebrities and civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison during a news conference set for Wednesday in downtown Los Angeles as prosecutors review new evidence to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their pare
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Unions face a moment of truth in Michigan in this year's presidential race
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump are appealing to middle-class workers in very different ways.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The son of a South Carolina inmate urges the governor to save his father from execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Richard Moore never meant to kill anyone the night he robbed a South Carolina convenience store and the Black man was convicted by a jury with no African Americans, his son and lawyers say as they fight to save the inmate from execution next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of troops kicked out under 'don't ask, don't tell' get upgraded to honorable discharges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon announced Tuesday that more than 800 military personnel have seen their service records upgraded to honorable discharges after previously being kicked out of the military under its former “don’t ask, don’t tell” policy.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho will begin using deep veins as backup for lethal injection executions, officials say
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho will begin using central veins deep in the groin, neck, chest or arm for executions by lethal injection if attempts to insert standard IV lines fail, the Idaho Department of Correction announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illegal immigrants arrested in wealthy Florida county for sexual crimes against a child
item.description: A trio of illegal immigrants were arrested in Palm Beach County, Florida, last week for committing sexual crimes against a child, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DNA forensics helps identify remains found in Colorado freezer as teenager missing for nearly 20 years
item.description: The Mesa County Sheriff's Office announced that remains found inside a freezer in January have been identified as those from a 16-year-old girl who went missing in 2005.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane misinformation stokes fears ahead of election
item.description: The flood of conspiracy theories and rumors following the severe weather in the South is sparking concerns that political figures’ embrace of misinformation is just the beginning as the general election draws closer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania has embarked on a historic journey across the Pacific Ocean from San Diego to Australia in a solo rowing boat, aiming to be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000 kilometer trip alone, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, KRON4

item.title: Stan Musial Bridge closes for two days over building fire
item.description: ST. LOUIS - A massive fire is wreaking havoc at an abandoned building in St. Louis. Crews with IDOT and MoDOT worked with the St. Louis Fire Department to shut down the Stan Musial Bridge Monday due to a building fire directly next to Interstate 70.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected 'murder hornet' found in Washington State
item.description: The northern giant hornet, sometimes nicknamed the "murder hornet," has been known to attack and kill entire honeybee hives.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two men shot during Pennsylvania assassination attempt on Trump say Secret Service failed them
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Two men who were shot during the first assassination attempt on Donald Trump this summer say the U.S. Secret Service was “negligent” in protecting the former president and other bystanders at the campaign rally in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new wave of lawsuits
item.description: Sean “Diddy” Combs was hit Monday with a new wave of lawsuits accusing him of raping women, sexually assaulting men and molesting a 16-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jury selection underway in the trial of a man accused of killing 2 teen girls in 2017
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Eight women and six men have been selected so far to a jury in the trial of a man charged in the Indiana killings of two teenage girls during a winter hike in 2017.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new lawsuits, including man who was 16 at the time
item.description: Twice denied bail, the Bad Boy Records founder remains locked up a Brooklyn federal jail while awaiting trial in May.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Which are the most popular Halloween candies in Utah?
item.description: Are you hoping the neighborhood mischief-makers won’t “T.P.” your house on Halloween? Then maybe you should hand out the candies they actually like.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: NJ Transit operator killed, passengers injured after train hits tree
item.description: An NJ Transit train operator is dead and nearly two dozen passengers are injured after a train struck a tree on the tracks Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, ABC 4

item.title: Members of the Kennedy family gather for funeral of Ethel Kennedy
item.description: CENTERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — Members of the Kennedy family gathered Monday for the funeral of Ethel Kennedy, the wife of the late Sen. Robert F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Aidan Hutchinson likely down 4-6 months with broken leg, Lions coach Dan Campbell says
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Aidan Hutchinson is likely done playing this year because of the leg injury he sustained against the Cowboys, but surgery to repair his fractured tibia and fibula “went great” and Lions coach Dan Campbell said Monday he isn't ready to declare his star pass rusher out for the
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FDA shakes up weight loss market as Mounjaro shortage ends
item.description: Federal regulators shook up the booming weight loss drug marketplace when they announced Mounjaro and Zepbound to be no longer in shortage after nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: List: Here are the nearly 500 Big Lots stores that have or will be closed this year
item.description: Since filing for bankruptcy protection earlier this year, Big Lots has slated nearly 500 stores for closure - some of which have already closed their doors for good.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Jerry West becomes first three-time inductee as Carter and Billups enter basketball's Hall of Fame
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Mass. (AP) — The stage was filled with Laker greats when Jerry West made history Sunday night by becoming the first three-time inductee to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Middle East latest: US says it will send THAAD defense battery to Israel, along with US troops
item.description: The U.S. is sending a Terminal High Altitude Area Defense battery to Israel, along with US troops to operate it. The Pentagon made the announcement just hours after Iran warned the U.S. to keep its military forces out of Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida neighbors band together to recover after one-two punch from hurricanes Helene and Milton
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — When ankle-deep floodwaters from Hurricane Helene bubbled up through the floors of their home, Kat Robinson-Malone and her husband sent a late-night text message to their neighbors two doors down: “Hey, we're coming.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and LAURA BARGFELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Breanna Stewart helps New York even WNBA Finals series as Liberty beat Lynx 80-66 in Game 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Breanna Stewart wasn't going to let history repeat itself. With the Minnesota Lynx making another fourth quarter run, the two-time league MVP refused to let the New York Liberty lose again.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Deputies 'probably' prevented 3rd Trump assassination attempt, California sheriff says
item.description: A man was arrested outside of a campaign rally for former President Donald Trump in Southern California, and multiple guns were illegally in his possession, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Professor leaves university after 'highly inappropriate' remarks during lecture
item.description: A University of Kansas professor has left the university after a video went viral on X showing the professor making unnerving remarks
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, WFLA, NBC
